description: The product supports discrete event simulation as a first-class modeling paradigm, where state changes occur at discrete points in time triggered by scheduled events.
The product supports agent-based modeling, where autonomous agents interact according to rules and produce emergent system behavior.
The product supports system dynamics modeling with stocks, flows, feedback loops, and continuous differential equations.
The product supports continuous simulation via differential equations or continuous state variables, not just discrete event scheduling.
The product supports pure Monte Carlo simulation for statistical sampling and uncertainty quantification, distinct from time-based discrete event simulation.
The product supports models that combine multiple paradigms (e.g., discrete event + agent-based + system dynamics) in the same simulation.
Models are built primarily by dragging visual components from a palette onto a canvas, connecting them, and configuring properties through forms — with little or no code required.
Models are built primarily by writing code in a scripting or programming language, with limited or no visual authoring. Common in open-source libraries like SimPy.
Models are built by drawing or authoring process flow diagrams (BPMN, flowchart, or custom notation) that are then interpreted or translated into simulation logic.
Models are authored in a proprietary or domain-specific language designed for simulation (e.g., SIMAN in Arena, SimTalk in Plant Simulation, FlexScript in FlexSim).
The product supports authoring or extending models using Python code.
The product supports authoring or extending models using Java code.
The product supports authoring or extending models using C# code.
The product supports authoring or extending models using JavaScript or TypeScript code.
The product supports authoring or extending models using Visual Basic for Applications (VBA).
The product supports 2D animation of entity flow through the simulation, visible during execution or in replay.
The product supports 3D animation with spatial positioning, camera controls, and realistic visualization of physical systems.
The product produces static charts, reports, and plots of simulation output (histograms, time series, utilization reports).
The product supports live dashboards that update during simulation execution — gauges, counters, real-time charts.
The product has built-in or tightly integrated optimization (e.g., OptQuest, genetic algorithms, gradient-based) for parameter tuning and scenario optimization.
The product has built-in support for factorial, fractional factorial, or other structured DOE techniques for systematic scenario comparison.
The product has built-in statistical analysis of simulation output — confidence intervals, warm-up detection, replication analysis, comparison tests.
The product can read from and write to external databases (SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, etc.) for parameter loading and output persistence.
The product exposes a programmatic API (library, REST, or IPC) that allows external code to control the simulation, load models, run experiments, or retrieve results.
The product integrates with general-purpose diagramming tools (LucidChart, Miro, diagrams.net, Visio) to import or author models from diagrams drawn in those tools.
